message:
feat(mvcc): extract WAL replay engine and harden recovery pipeline

Introduce WalReplayEngine (wal_replay.rs, 807 lines) — a standalone,
policy-driven replay module that decouples WAL record iteration from
the MVCC store.  Two TailPolicy variants control corruption handling:
TruncateToLastGood (production default) and FailFast (audit/strict).
Each replay now produces a classified ReplayOutcome (Clean, EmptyLog,
TruncatedTail, CorruptTail, MonotonicityViolation) surfaced in the
WalRecoveryReport.

persist.rs shrinks by ~50 lines: the hand-rolled decode loop is
replaced by a WalReplayEngine::replay() call with an apply closure,
making the replay path independently testable and fuzzable.

Additional cleanup across wal_writer.rs and benchmarks:
- PersistOptions parameters changed to &PersistOptions (borrow, not move)
- SyncPolicy derives Default via #[default] attribute instead of manual impl
- FfsError From conversions use Self:: shorthand
- #[must_use] added to WalWriter::new and WalWriter::file
- Reformatted chained calls and map_err closures (rustfmt)

New E2E scripts: ffs_wal_replay_e2e.sh and ffs_wal_writer_e2e.sh
for structured verification of the replay engine and writer contracts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-h6nz","title":"EPIC: V1.1 Post-Parity Hardening and Operationalization","description":"# EPIC: V1.1 Post-Parity Hardening and Operationalization

## Context
Tracked V1 parity is complete (75/75), but FrankenFS is explicitly still early-stage and experimental for mount/write operations. The project now needs a rigorous post-parity program focused on production-grade correctness confidence, failure containment, observability, and predictable performance under stress.

This epic converts that post-parity intent into an executable dependency graph that is:
- granular enough for independent agent execution,
- explicit about acceptance gates,
- self-contained (no need to reopen planning docs during execution).

## Why this exists
Parity completion proves breadth of implemented behavior, not necessarily depth of operational hardening. Remaining risk lives in:
- durability under crash/fault scenarios,
- clarity of experimental behavior (especially btrfs RW path),
- incomplete operational wiring (CLI mount path for managed backpressure/per-core hooks),
- benchmark/regression signal fidelity,
- unresolved spec open questions that can cause architecture drift if left ambiguous.

## Scope
This epic is fulfilled when all child epics below are complete:
1. Durable MVCC version-store implementation and replay hardening.
2. CLI mount wiring for managed backpressure/per-core scheduling.
3. btrfs experimental RW hardening and explicit compatibility contracts.
4. Fuzzing and adversarial test-depth expansion.
5. Benchmark fidelity and regression-guard upgrades.
6. Closure of formal open questions (with accepted decisions integrated into docs/tests/code).

## Non-goals
- Reopening excluded V1 scope items (multi-device btrfs, fscrypt, inline_data, etc.) unless explicitly approved in a separate scope-change epic.
- Marketing/docs polish without technical acceptance deltas.

## Definition of Done
- Child epics closed with evidence-backed acceptance criteria.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md, spec docs, and harness signals remain internally consistent.
- New work items include explicit test and observability requirements, not implementation-only checklists.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:48:35.532955522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:04:12.361294363Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_program_root"],"comments":[{"id":294,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution sequencing recommendation (to avoid coordination deadlocks):
1) Start with `bd-h6nz.6.*` OQ closures that define policy boundaries.
2) In parallel, advance implementation-ready tracks that already have clear contracts (`bd-h6nz.2.*`, `bd-h6nz.4.*`, `bd-h6nz.5.*`).
3) Begin durable MVCC implementation (`bd-h6nz.1.*`) once OQ7/OQ4 decisions are available where required.
4) Use `bd-h6nz.7.*` after telemetry and behavior contracts are stable.

Reasoning:
- This order minimizes rework from policy churn.
- It keeps high-throughput execution by allowing independent tracks to proceed in parallel.
- It prevents communication purgatory by giving every agent a concrete lane with clear dependencies.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:55:07Z"},{"id":347,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space optimization note (2026-03-03):\n- Removed over-broad cross-epic blockers (bd-h6nz.3<-bd-h6nz.6.3, bd-h6nz.5<-bd-h6nz.2, bd-h6nz.7<-{bd-h6nz.1,bd-h6nz.2,bd-h6nz.3}) to increase safe parallelism and avoid blocking noise.\n- Kept correctness constraints at task-level where they are actually needed (e.g., bd-h6nz.3.4 still depends on bd-h6nz.6.3).\n- Strengthened benchmark gate coupling with mount-mode gate (bd-h6nz.5.6 depends on bd-h6nz.2.6).\n- Added telemetry dependency for degraded-runtime benchmarking (bd-h6nz.5.4 depends on bd-h6nz.2.4).\nRationale: maximize throughput in plan space without reducing scope or lowering acceptance quality.","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:04:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-h6nz.1","title":"EPIC: Durable MVCC Version Store and Crash Replay","description":"# EPIC: Durable MVCC Version Store and Crash Replay

## Problem Statement
MVCC currently provides strong in-memory isolation and conflict semantics, but the long-term architecture calls for a durable version-store path with restart replay guarantees. This is explicitly documented as planned in the spec and is a key reliability boundary between "experimental" and "operationally trustworthy" write paths.

## Why this matters
Without a durable MVCC version-store:
- commit visibility across process restart is less explicit,
- crash-time reconstruction relies on narrower recovery channels,
- validation of full write-path durability semantics remains incomplete.

## Scope
Implement and harden a persistent MVCC version-store workflow including:
1. append protocol and on-disk record format,
2. replay engine and corruption handling,
3. mount/open integration + observability,
4. deterministic crash matrix coverage.

## Required Evidence
- deterministic replay tests proving latest committed state reconstruction,
- corrupted/truncated log handling tests with explicit outcomes,
- metrics and CLI visibility for replay/apply/skip decisions.

## Done Criteria
- Child tasks completed with green gates and replay evidence.
- Spec/architecture docs updated from "planned" to implemented behavior.
- No silent recovery ambiguity (each replay path emits a reasoned outcome).","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:48:43.695973833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:59:55.756410424Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_mvcc_durable"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:48:43.695973833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":295,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self note:
Treat this epic as a correctness program, not a feature program. Every step should produce replay-oracle evidence. If a design choice cannot be tested deterministically under crash schedules, it is not ready to ship.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:55:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-h6nz.1.1","title":"Define durable MVCC version-store record format and invariants","description":"## Objective
Turn the planned durable MVCC record model into an implementable contract with no ambiguity around ordering, checksums, atomicity boundaries, and forward compatibility.

## Background
Spec section 5.9 defines high-level goals. This task crystallizes the concrete wire/storage format and invariant ledger required before implementation.

## Deliverables
1. Record schema for version-store entries (header + payload + integrity fields).
2. Explicit ordering model (commit_seq monotonicity, tie-breaking, duplicate handling).
3. Crash consistency points (what is guaranteed after power loss at each append stage).
4. Corruption semantics (truncate vs reject vs quarantine behavior).
5. Compatibility/versioning strategy for future schema evolution.

## Required Artifacts
- Spec updates in COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md + PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Invariant checklist suitable for test oracles.
- Example binary layout snippets and decode walkthrough.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No undecided fields in the record layout.
- Replay behavior is fully deterministic for all malformed/torn cases.
- At least 5 explicit invariants are documented and test-mappable.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:34.532430678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-04T06:40:03.819178840Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T06:40:03.819155396Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_mvcc_durable","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:34.532430678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":305,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space revision (quality contract, mandatory before closure):

1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add/extend crate-local unit tests for happy path, edge/boundary cases, and error paths.
- Include at least one negative/invariant test proving the failure mode is handled intentionally.

2. E2E Scripts (required)
- Add/extend deterministic E2E coverage under scripts/e2e/ (or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- E2E output must include machine-parseable pass/fail markers and scenario identifiers.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Add structured logs at key state transitions and error boundaries (debug/info/warn/error as appropriate).
- Log fields must include operation/scenario identifiers so incidents are traceable.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) that verify critical log markers are emitted for success and failure paths.

4. Validation Gates (required)
- Run via rch where applicable: cargo fmt/check/clippy/test + targeted E2E script(s).
- Closure comment must summarize test evidence and logging-evidence locations.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:58:37Z"},{"id":351,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed spec contract for durable MVCC version-store record format and invariants.\n\nArtifacts:\n- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md §5.9.2..§5.9.10\n- PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md §3.2.1\n\nKey outcomes:\n1) Replaced abstract VERSION/COMMIT marker sketch with concrete commit-record wire format (file header + commit framing + write entries + CRC scope).\n2) Defined canonical serialization rules (sorted block order, single write per block, reserved sentinel IDs).\n3) Added crash-consistency matrix and replay truncation/corruption policy with deterministic monotonicity checks.\n4) Added compatibility/versioning rules for WAL evolution.\n5) Added 8 explicit durable invariants (D1..D8) and a stable validation matrix with scenario IDs MVCC_DURABLE_WAL_001..006 plus required structured log fields.\n\nThis closes bd-h6nz.1.1 acceptance criteria (no undecided layout fields, deterministic malformed-tail replay behavior, and >=5 test-mappable invariants).","created_at":"2026-03-04T06:40:00Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-h6nz.1.2","title":"Implement append-only durable MVCC log writer with integrity checks","description":"## Objective
Implement the write path for durable version-store records in `ffs-mvcc`, including checksum emission, append atomicity discipline, and bounded fsync strategy.

## Scope
1. Writer API for staging + append + durability boundary.
2. Per-record integrity fields and verification-on-write.
3. Batching policy with explicit tradeoffs (latency vs durability).
4. Backpressure integration when persistence path saturates.

## Guardrails
- No silent drop of records on partial I/O.
- Distinguish retryable I/O errors vs fatal format/invariant violations.
- Emit structured logs for append lifecycle and failure classes.

## Tests
- Unit tests for normal append, short write, torn write simulation, checksum mismatch.
- Property test for monotonic ordering preservation across randomized append sequences.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Appended records decode correctly by a strict reader.
- Failure cases return explicit typed errors (not generic format strings).
- `cargo test -p ffs-mvcc` includes deterministic coverage for append failure modes.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:41.446794124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T05:15:36.800028944Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_mvcc_durable","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:41.446794124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:41.446794124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:53:35.129766204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":304,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space revision (quality contract, mandatory before closure):

1. Unit Tests (required)

- Include at least one negative/invariant test proving the failure mode is handled intentionally.

2. E2E Scripts (required)
- Add/extend deterministic E2E coverage under scripts/e2e/ (or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- E2E output must include machine-parseable pass/fail markers and scenario identifiers.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Add structured logs at key state transitions and error boundaries (debug/info/warn/error as appropriate).
- Log fields must include operation/scenario identifiers so incidents are traceable.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) that verify critical log markers are emitted for success and failure paths.

4. Validation Gates (required)
- Run via rch where applicable: cargo fmt/check/clippy/test + targeted E2E script(s).
- Closure comment must summarize test evidence and logging-evidence locations.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:58:37Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-h6nz.1.3","title":"Implement durable MVCC replay engine with corruption/torn-record recovery policy","description":"## Objective
Build restart replay for durable version-store records, producing deterministic reconstruction of latest committed visibility while handling partial/corrupt tails safely.

## Scope
1. Replay scanner and decoder with strict validation.
2. Tail-handling policy (truncate-to-last-good vs fail-fast with operator override).
3. Replay outcome classification (`clean`, `truncated_tail`, `corrupt_abort`, etc.).
4. Integration into MVCC in-memory structures without violating snapshot invariants.

## Tests
- Replay from clean log.
- Replay with truncated final record.
- Replay with mid-log corruption.
- Replay idempotence (multiple replays yield identical state).
- Replay ordering correctness across randomized commit sequences.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Replay outcomes are explicit and auditable.
- Invariant checks after replay pass for all supported scenarios.
- Corruption handling never produces silent partial visibility.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:47.497189623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:06:07.886203166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_mvcc_durable","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:47.497189623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:47.497189623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:47.497189623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":303,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space revision (quality contract, mandatory before closure):

- Include at least one negative/invariant test proving the failure mode is handled intentionally.

2. E2E Scripts (required)
- Add/extend deterministic E2E coverage under scripts/e2e/ (or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- E2E output must include machine-parseable pass/fail markers and scenario identifiers.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Add structured logs at key state transitions and error boundaries (debug/info/warn/error as appropriate).
- Log fields must include operation/scenario identifiers so incidents are traceable.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) that verify critical log markers are emitted for success and failure paths.

4. Validation Gates (required)
- Run via rch where applicable: cargo fmt/check/clippy/test + targeted E2E script(s).
- Closure comment must summarize test evidence and logging-evidence locations.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:58:37Z"}]}

- Distinguish retryable I/O errors vs fatal format/invariant violations.
- Emit structured logs for append lifecycle and failure classes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Failure cases return explicit typed errors (not generic format strings).
- `cargo test -p ffs-mvcc` includes deterministic coverage for append failure modes.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)

- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.

- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:41.446794124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:06:19.416577449Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:06:19.415851268Z","close_reason":"Implemented WalWriter append-only durable MVCC log writer. New module: crates/ffs-mvcc/src/wal_writer.rs with WalWriter, WalWriteError (typed error classification: retryable vs fatal), SyncPolicy (Immediate/EveryN/Manual), WalWriterConfig (verify_writes, backpressure_threshold_bytes), AppendResult. Enforces D1 (monotonicity) and D8 (sentinel rejection). Structured logging at all state transitions (wal_append_start/ok/err, wal_sync_ok/err, wal_backpressure, wal_verify_ok/err, wal_flush_ok). PersistentMvccStore now uses WalWriter instead of internal WalFile. 20 new unit tests + 1 property test (proptest_monotonic_ordering_preserved). E2E: scripts/e2e/ffs_wal_writer_e2e.sh (11 scenarios). All 335 tests pass, fmt clean, clippy clean (-D warnings), workspace compiles.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_mvcc_durable","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:41.446794124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:41.446794124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:53:35.129766204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":304,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space revision (quality contract, mandatory before closure):

+{"id":"bd-h6nz.1.3","title":"Implement durable MVCC replay engine with corruption/torn-record recovery policy","description":"## Objective
Build restart replay for durable version-store records, producing deterministic reconstruction of latest committed visibility while handling partial/corrupt tails safely.

## Scope
1. Replay scanner and decoder with strict validation.
2. Tail-handling policy (truncate-to-last-good vs fail-fast with operator override).
3. Replay outcome classification (`clean`, `truncated_tail`, `corrupt_abort`, etc.).
4. Integration into MVCC in-memory structures without violating snapshot invariants.

## Tests
- Replay from clean log.
- Replay with truncated final record.
- Replay with mid-log corruption.
- Replay idempotence (multiple replays yield identical state).
- Replay ordering correctness across randomized commit sequences.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Replay outcomes are explicit and auditable.
- Invariant checks after replay pass for all supported scenarios.
- Corruption handling never produces silent partial visibility.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)

- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)

- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)

- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.1.4","title":"Integrate durable MVCC store into OpenFs lifecycle and mount options","description":"## Objective
Wire durable MVCC persistence and replay into `OpenFs::open` / mount lifecycle with explicit mode controls and safe defaults.

## Scope
1. Open-time replay invocation and replay summary capture.
2. Mode flags for strict vs tolerant replay handling.
3. Read-only fallback behavior when replay policy requires it.
4. Integration with existing journal/recovery reporting surfaces.

## Constraints
- No hidden behavior changes for default read-only mount semantics.
- Replay failure modes must map to actionable CLI errors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mount/open path clearly reports replay decisions.
- Recovery + replay ordering is deterministic and documented.
- Integration tests cover clean start, crash restart, and replay-failure paths.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:53.857224323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:06:08.112002090Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_mvcc_durable","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:53.857224323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:53.857224323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:53.857224323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:53:35.339260363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":325,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space revision (quality contract, mandatory before closure):

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.1.5","title":"Build deterministic crash/restart matrix for durable MVCC replay correctness","description":"## Objective
Prove replay correctness under adversarial crash timing using deterministic schedule exploration and fault injection.

## Scope
1. Crash points around append/fsync/commit boundaries.
2. Replay validation oracle for visible state and invariants.
3. Matrix report of tested crash schedules and outcomes.

## Required Test Classes
- crash-before-record-visible
- crash-after-record-before-checksum
- crash-after-checksum-before-sync
- crash-after-sync-before-commit-seq-publish
- repeated crash/replay cycles

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic matrix run is reproducible by seed.
- No schedule yields silent divergence from oracle state.
- Results are summarized in machine-readable artifact for CI trend tracking.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:59.871483994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:06:08.342887974Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_mvcc_durable","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:59.871483994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:59.871483994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:59.871483994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":324,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space revision (quality contract, mandatory before closure):

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# ffs_wal_replay_e2e.sh - E2E verification for durable MVCC replay engine (bd-h6nz.1.3)
+#
+# Validates that:
+# 1. WalReplayEngine type exists and is public
+# 2. TailPolicy and ReplayOutcome enums exist
+# 3. Replay engine unit tests pass (clean, truncated, corrupt, FailFast, etc.)
+# 4. Persist integration tests pass (replay through PersistentMvccStore)
+# 5. Structured logging events are emitted
+# 6. Replay outcome classification is correct
+# 7. Idempotent replay produces identical results
+#
+# Usage: ./scripts/e2e/ffs_wal_replay_e2e.sh
+#
+# Exit codes:
+#   0 - All scenarios passed
+#   1 - One or more scenarios failed
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.."
+REPO_ROOT="$(pwd)"
+export REPO_ROOT
+
+# Source shared helpers
+source "$REPO_ROOT/scripts/e2e/lib.sh"
+
+export RUST_LOG="${RUST_LOG:-info}"
+export RUST_BACKTRACE="${RUST_BACKTRACE:-1}"
+
+PASS_COUNT=0
+FAIL_COUNT=0
+TOTAL=0
+
+scenario_result() {
+    local scenario_id="$1"
+    local status="$2"
+    local detail="$3"
+    e2e_log "SCENARIO_RESULT|scenario_id=${scenario_id}|status=${status}|detail=${detail}"
+    if [[ "$status" == "PASS" ]]; then
+        PASS_COUNT=$((PASS_COUNT + 1))
+    else
+        FAIL_COUNT=$((FAIL_COUNT + 1))
+    fi
+    TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
+}
+
+e2e_init "ffs_wal_replay"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 1: Replay engine types exist and are public
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 1: Replay engine type existence"
+
+TYPES_FOUND=0
+for item in "pub enum TailPolicy" "pub enum ReplayOutcome" "pub struct ReplayReport" "pub struct WalReplayEngine"; do
+    if grep -q "$item" crates/ffs-mvcc/src/wal_replay.rs; then
+        TYPES_FOUND=$((TYPES_FOUND + 1))
+    fi
+done
+
+if [[ $TYPES_FOUND -eq 4 ]]; then
+    scenario_result "replay_engine_types" "PASS" "All 4 public types present"
+else
+    scenario_result "replay_engine_types" "FAIL" "Only ${TYPES_FOUND}/4 public types found"
+fi
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 2: TailPolicy variants
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 2: TailPolicy variants"
+
+VARIANTS_FOUND=0
+for variant in "TruncateToLastGood" "FailFast"; do
+    if grep -q "$variant" crates/ffs-mvcc/src/wal_replay.rs; then
+        VARIANTS_FOUND=$((VARIANTS_FOUND + 1))
+    fi
+done
+
+if [[ $VARIANTS_FOUND -eq 2 ]]; then
+    scenario_result "replay_tail_policy" "PASS" "Both TailPolicy variants present"
+else
+    scenario_result "replay_tail_policy" "FAIL" "Only ${VARIANTS_FOUND}/2 TailPolicy variants found"
+fi
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 3: ReplayOutcome variants
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 3: ReplayOutcome variants"
+
+OUTCOME_FOUND=0
+for variant in "Clean" "EmptyLog" "TruncatedTail" "CorruptTail" "MonotonicityViolation"; do
+    if grep -q "$variant" crates/ffs-mvcc/src/wal_replay.rs; then
+        OUTCOME_FOUND=$((OUTCOME_FOUND + 1))
+    fi
+done
+
+if [[ $OUTCOME_FOUND -eq 5 ]]; then
+    scenario_result "replay_outcome_variants" "PASS" "All 5 ReplayOutcome variants present"
+else
+    scenario_result "replay_outcome_variants" "FAIL" "Only ${OUTCOME_FOUND}/5 ReplayOutcome variants found"
+fi
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 4: Replay engine unit tests pass
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 4: Replay engine unit tests"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_replay::tests 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    TESTS_RUN=$(grep -c "test wal_replay::tests" "$TEST_LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+    if [[ $TESTS_RUN -ge 15 ]]; then
+        scenario_result "replay_engine_unit" "PASS" "Replay engine unit tests passed (${TESTS_RUN} tests)"
+    else
+        scenario_result "replay_engine_unit" "FAIL" "Too few tests: ${TESTS_RUN} (expected >= 15)"
+    fi
+else
+    scenario_result "replay_engine_unit" "FAIL" "Replay engine unit tests failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 5: Truncated tail handling tests
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 5: Truncated tail handling"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_replay::tests::replay_truncated 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    scenario_result "replay_truncated_tail" "PASS" "Truncated tail tests passed"
+else
+    scenario_result "replay_truncated_tail" "FAIL" "Truncated tail tests failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 6: FailFast policy tests
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 6: FailFast policy"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_replay::tests::replay_corrupt_crc_fail_fast 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    scenario_result "replay_fail_fast" "PASS" "FailFast policy tests passed"
+else
+    scenario_result "replay_fail_fast" "FAIL" "FailFast policy tests failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 7: Monotonicity enforcement
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 7: Monotonicity enforcement"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_replay::tests::replay_rejects 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    scenario_result "replay_monotonicity" "PASS" "Monotonicity enforcement tests passed"
+else
+    scenario_result "replay_monotonicity" "FAIL" "Monotonicity enforcement tests failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 8: Idempotent replay
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 8: Idempotent replay"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_replay::tests::replay_is_idempotent 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    scenario_result "replay_idempotent" "PASS" "Idempotent replay test passed"
+else
+    scenario_result "replay_idempotent" "FAIL" "Idempotent replay test failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 9: PersistentMvccStore integration (uses replay engine)
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 9: PersistentMvccStore integration"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- persist::tests 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    PERSIST_TESTS=$(grep -c "test persist::tests" "$TEST_LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+    scenario_result "replay_persist_integration" "PASS" "PersistentMvccStore tests passed (${PERSIST_TESTS} tests)"
+else
+    scenario_result "replay_persist_integration" "FAIL" "PersistentMvccStore tests failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 10: Structured logging markers present
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 10: Structured logging markers"
+
+LOG_MARKERS_FOUND=0
+for marker in "wal_replay_start" "wal_replay_done" "wal_replay_apply" "wal_replay_empty" "wal_replay_end_of_data" "wal_replay_truncated_tail" "wal_replay_corrupt_tail" "wal_replay_monotonicity_violation" "wal_replay_sentinel_rejected"; do
+    if grep -q "\"${marker}\"" crates/ffs-mvcc/src/wal_replay.rs; then
+        LOG_MARKERS_FOUND=$((LOG_MARKERS_FOUND + 1))
+    fi
+done
+
+if [[ $LOG_MARKERS_FOUND -ge 7 ]]; then
+    scenario_result "replay_logging" "PASS" "Structured logging: ${LOG_MARKERS_FOUND}/9 markers present"
+else
+    scenario_result "replay_logging" "FAIL" "Only ${LOG_MARKERS_FOUND}/9 structured log markers found"
+fi
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 11: ReplayOutcome integrated into WalRecoveryReport
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 11: ReplayOutcome in WalRecoveryReport"
+
+if grep -q "pub outcome: ReplayOutcome" crates/ffs-mvcc/src/persist.rs; then
+    scenario_result "replay_outcome_in_report" "PASS" "ReplayOutcome field present in WalRecoveryReport"
+else
+    scenario_result "replay_outcome_in_report" "FAIL" "ReplayOutcome field missing from WalRecoveryReport"
+fi
+
+#######################################
+# Summary
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Summary"
+e2e_log "Results: ${PASS_COUNT}/${TOTAL} PASS, ${FAIL_COUNT}/${TOTAL} FAIL"
+
+if [[ $FAIL_COUNT -gt 0 ]]; then
+    e2e_log "OVERALL: FAIL"
+    exit 1
+else
+    e2e_log "OVERALL: PASS"
+    exit 0
+fi

@@ -0,0 +1,222 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# ffs_wal_writer_e2e.sh - E2E verification for append-only WAL writer (bd-h6nz.1.2)
+#
+# Validates that:
+# 1. WalWriter type exists and is public
+# 2. WalWriter unit tests pass (append, monotonicity, backpressure, etc.)
+# 3. PersistentMvccStore integration tests pass (commit, replay, rollback)
+# 4. Property tests pass (monotonic ordering preservation)
+# 5. Structured logging events are emitted
+# 6. Error classification is correct (retryable vs fatal)
+# 7. Sync policy variants work correctly
+#
+# Usage: ./scripts/e2e/ffs_wal_writer_e2e.sh
+#
+# Exit codes:
+#   0 - All scenarios passed
+#   1 - One or more scenarios failed
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.."
+REPO_ROOT="$(pwd)"
+export REPO_ROOT
+
+# Source shared helpers
+source "$REPO_ROOT/scripts/e2e/lib.sh"
+
+export RUST_LOG="${RUST_LOG:-info}"
+export RUST_BACKTRACE="${RUST_BACKTRACE:-1}"
+
+PASS_COUNT=0
+FAIL_COUNT=0
+TOTAL=0
+
+scenario_result() {
+    local scenario_id="$1"
+    local status="$2"
+    local detail="$3"
+    e2e_log "SCENARIO_RESULT|scenario_id=${scenario_id}|status=${status}|detail=${detail}"
+    if [[ "$status" == "PASS" ]]; then
+        PASS_COUNT=$((PASS_COUNT + 1))
+    else
+        FAIL_COUNT=$((FAIL_COUNT + 1))
+    fi
+    TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
+}
+
+e2e_init "ffs_wal_writer"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 1: WalWriter type exists and is public
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 1: WalWriter type existence"
+
+TYPES_FOUND=0
+for item in "pub struct WalWriter" "pub enum WalWriteError" "pub enum SyncPolicy" "pub struct WalWriterConfig" "pub struct AppendResult"; do
+    if grep -q "$item" crates/ffs-mvcc/src/wal_writer.rs; then
+        TYPES_FOUND=$((TYPES_FOUND + 1))
+    fi
+done
+
+if [[ $TYPES_FOUND -eq 5 ]]; then
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_types" "PASS" "All 5 public types present"
+else
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_types" "FAIL" "Only ${TYPES_FOUND}/5 public types found"
+fi
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 2: WalWriter unit tests pass
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 2: WalWriter unit tests"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_writer::tests 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    TESTS_RUN=$(grep -c "test wal_writer::tests" "$TEST_LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+    if [[ $TESTS_RUN -ge 15 ]]; then
+        scenario_result "wal_writer_unit" "PASS" "WalWriter unit tests passed (${TESTS_RUN} tests)"
+    else
+        scenario_result "wal_writer_unit" "FAIL" "Too few WalWriter tests: ${TESTS_RUN} (expected >= 15)"
+    fi
+else
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_unit" "FAIL" "WalWriter unit tests failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 3: Monotonicity enforcement tests
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 3: Monotonicity enforcement"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_writer::tests::rejects_non_monotonic 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_monotonic" "PASS" "Monotonicity enforcement tests passed"
+else
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_monotonic" "FAIL" "Monotonicity enforcement tests failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 4: Sentinel rejection tests (D8)
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 4: Sentinel rejection (D8)"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_writer::tests::rejects_sentinel 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_sentinel" "PASS" "Sentinel rejection tests passed"
+else
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_sentinel" "FAIL" "Sentinel rejection tests failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 5: Backpressure threshold
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 5: Backpressure threshold"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_writer::tests::backpressure 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_backpressure" "PASS" "Backpressure threshold tests passed"
+else
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_backpressure" "FAIL" "Backpressure threshold tests failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 6: Sync policy variants
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 6: Sync policy variants"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_writer::tests::sync_policy 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    SYNC_TESTS=$(grep -c "test wal_writer::tests::sync_policy" "$TEST_LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_sync_policy" "PASS" "Sync policy tests passed (${SYNC_TESTS} variants)"
+else
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_sync_policy" "FAIL" "Sync policy tests failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 7: Error classification
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 7: Error classification"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_writer::tests::error_classification 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_error_class" "PASS" "Error classification tests passed"
+else
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_error_class" "FAIL" "Error classification tests failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 8: Property test — monotonic ordering
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 8: Property test — monotonic ordering"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_writer::tests::proptest_monotonic 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_proptest_monotonic" "PASS" "Monotonic ordering property test passed"
+else
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_proptest_monotonic" "FAIL" "Monotonic ordering property test failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 9: PersistentMvccStore integration (uses WalWriter)
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 9: PersistentMvccStore integration"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- persist::tests 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    PERSIST_TESTS=$(grep -c "test persist::tests" "$TEST_LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_persist_integration" "PASS" "PersistentMvccStore tests passed (${PERSIST_TESTS} tests)"
+else
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_persist_integration" "FAIL" "PersistentMvccStore tests failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 10: Structured logging markers present
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 10: Structured logging markers"
+
+LOG_MARKERS_FOUND=0
+for marker in "wal_append_start" "wal_append_ok" "wal_sync_ok" "wal_append_err" "wal_sync_err" "wal_backpressure" "wal_verify_ok" "wal_verify_err" "wal_flush_ok"; do
+    if grep -q "\"${marker}\"" crates/ffs-mvcc/src/wal_writer.rs; then
+        LOG_MARKERS_FOUND=$((LOG_MARKERS_FOUND + 1))
+    fi
+done
+
+if [[ $LOG_MARKERS_FOUND -ge 7 ]]; then
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_logging" "PASS" "Structured logging: ${LOG_MARKERS_FOUND}/9 markers present"
+else
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_logging" "FAIL" "Only ${LOG_MARKERS_FOUND}/9 structured log markers found"
+fi
+
+#######################################
+# Scenario 11: Write verification (read-back) capability
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Scenario 11: Write verification"
+
+TEST_LOG=$(mktemp)
+if cargo test -p ffs-mvcc --lib -- wal_writer::tests::verify_writes 2>"$TEST_LOG" | tee -a "$TEST_LOG"; then
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_verify" "PASS" "Write verification test passed"
+else
+    scenario_result "wal_writer_verify" "FAIL" "Write verification test failed"
+fi
+rm -f "$TEST_LOG"
+
+#######################################
+# Summary
+#######################################
+e2e_step "Summary"
+e2e_log "Results: ${PASS_COUNT}/${TOTAL} PASS, ${FAIL_COUNT}/${TOTAL} FAIL"
+
+if [[ $FAIL_COUNT -gt 0 ]]; then
+    e2e_log "OVERALL: FAIL"
+    exit 1
+else
+    e2e_log "OVERALL: PASS"
+    exit 0
+fi
